reviews.review_msg: Lectures are entertaining and easy to follow but she makes lots of mistakes. Course material is pretty straight forward. As long as youre on top of practice questions you'll be fine.

She's very passionate about teaching but her lectures tend to be unorganized and she'll often make errors in her notes. Other than that, she's pretty good at teaching the course. Quizzes were simple and midterms were fair. Final exam was also significantly easier that the previous tests.

Marni is passionate about Linear Algebra, but she isn't the best at teaching. Lectures aren't organized very well, felt a little rushed most of the times. Tests are easy and reasonable. Actually study for your quizzes, understand the concepts, and you will be fine.

Right before the final as I walked by her she looked me in the eyes and then started talking to a TA about how all the calculus students had a dead look in their eyes. Her lectures were pretty good, engaged with the students a lot, and I felt like the tests were fair. Only giving a low rating because i am mad i had to write this final after calc.
Lectures are a little messy at times, highly recommended to do practice outside of class with the material. Quizzes taken directly from homework, very easy exams.

Bad professor. She was all over the place and wasn't very organized. Would have outburst middle of the class but that was mainly due to students talking. However, the tests were not to bad so if you studied properly you should do fine. Final was horrible. I think the average was a fail but unsure since she didnt release the class average for it.

I had a great experience in MATH157 with prof Mishna.  She would come to the ACW and help any students in there that didn't understand the concept taught in class.  She can sometimes be a bit frazzled during lectures or make a couple of errors but she is dyslexic lol (she told us when it happened like 8 times in a lecture).
She is the best teacher known to man. Would take her again!

She makes so many mistakes while teaching and she's not the best at explaining concepts. There was a lecture where 3 separate people asked her the same question and she could not answer it properly. I don't think I learned a thing from her all semester had to teach myself everything. Her midterms are fair but the final was crazy hard.

She is clearly very passionate about Math and this would sometimes mean that she would get into the theory a little more than needed. At first, I was a little nervous about how her exams may be but it turns out her Midterms were easier than expected and completely fair. However, the final was definitely challenging. Overall, nothing to be scared of

Dr. Mishna teaches one of those courses that just never gets boring. Calc 3 had very difficult content but she was always lighthearted and got us through it. She is always willing to help on homework questions if you are struggling. She is an amazing professor and an amazing person. If I see she is teaching a course I can take I would take it.

Thank you, Dr. Mishna. Dr. Mishna is really kind and friendly professor. The lectures were energetic thanks to the professor. This class was one of those few classes when you get to the end of the term, you'll start missing it that it's going to be finished soon. :(

Lets skip the prof. Why tf is Haggai as a TA again. The worst thing that Dr Mishna does is picking Haggai as a TA. He gives out 30% for the assignment again. His standard of marking assignments is just unreasonnable. I don't want to see Haggai in my life. Wish the school fire him. He doesn't even know what is math.

Dr. Mishna is definitely one of the better math profs at SFU. She is very passionate about the subject and that carries over into her teaching. She is very lenient when grading exams (and also grades the exams quickly!). Lectures sometimes felt disorganized and difficult to follow, but for the most part she is a clear and enthusiastic lecturer.

I dont like her at all. Every lecture she just copy at least 5 pages of notes. Notes are messy. She always talked to herself assume that is teaching. Exams was hard, tried to throw everything to us in one hour class. Hate Her!

Fantastic lectures, for once I preferred my math prof's explanations over those of the textbook. Lots of homework but she just wants to force you to understand the content of the class in-depth. Gave a make-up exam and curved my grade up on the final after it became apparent that people struggled with a certain chapter. Overall excellent professor
She takes the time to work through things with her students and is very willing to meet and discuss material and marks

Dr. Marni Mishna is a great professor. She explained her lectures very clear with working on examples in details. She was very honest if she herself was confused about some concepts, but she was able to work on them and explain clearly. It was very amazing that she inspired and encouraged students to ask questions. She was serious and kind.

You need to continuously review the textbook to understand the topics, as lectures can be quite confusing (the textbook itself was quite confusing too).  Exams only tested the easier aspects of the course, so they were pretty straightforward.  Overall a pretty decent prof

Goes EXTREMELY fast in her lectures. However, she is helpful in the algebra workshop. Make sure you understand and clarify any concepts because the next lecture is going to build off of the previous one.  Quizzes easy, first two midterms were fair, but final was extremely hard (the last third of the course is on another level in general).

here is the thing, you have to learn this course by yourself, or you will fail this course.  she didn't even know what she is talking about.  do not try to follow her lecture, 2 slides  two chapters. just study on your own. and you will get a great grade.

She tries way too hard to be funny when she should be more clear during her lectures. Seems rushed since most classes we got off about 7-10 min early. Sometimes doesn't even work through examples, which is frustrating because she says "try it yourself". Quizzes are really hard, but class is manageable.

The quizzes are grading tough but midterm and final are not. But still, exams are hard, although her teaching skill is good. She speak so fast but with very clear voice even I am a Chinese can easily understand.

I don't know if I have a bone to pick with the Prof, or SFU for making this class so difficult. Basically the amount of work involved in this class is ridiculous. It should be 2 courses because the class is not that difficult, and it's fundamental to anyone in Engineering of Computer Science. Her lectures are almost useless.

You need to spend time and understand the topics yourself as the lectures are not just enough. If you are not up to date with your stuff, you might be sitting in class and have no idea whats going on.
She is too fast in her lecture, does not care if we understand the material, all she care's about is finishing the material by the end of the semester.

The worst prof in SFU!! She just focus on some of those "PHYS" or "SUPER SCIENCE brain" students. The other students do not matter her at all. Even though our MT grade average are lower than 50%, it still dosn't give her any warning sign.

messy notes messy topics , hws are hard and no full solution posted, too much notes , approx 4-5 pages per hour, when u take 30s to think the previous sentence then you find another page of notes is done. lol
Decent prof, really into the course. Makes some mistakes on proofs that make it hard to follow, but encourages questions and takes the time to explain things.
She's very energetic but everything goes too fast. Exams are not really hard

She is a great prof! If you need to take a course with her, do it and you won't regret it. She is super interested in the course and improving the students' writing and skills. Her teaching is clear and questions are encouraged. She usually scales exams quite generously.

Great prof. Our class had 16 people in it, and she made the effort to make sure we all understood the material. She is very nice and can always explain the material to you in a different way if needed. The assignments and exams are on the difficult side, but she scales, ALOT. I highly recommend taking courses with her!

She is a nice prof, she tries hard to help student understand the material.  Keep in mind discrete math is not an easy material to grasp, so expect to spend loads of hours in the Algebra Workshop.  I did not learn anything during the lecture except copy loads of notes.  She writes notes on a Tablet but she refuses to put notes up online.
Nice prof, clear in-class notes and fantastic downloadable notes. The subject is pretty hard though, a lot of stuff crammed into one class.

She is a great prof. She seems very interested in the material she teaches and makes sure the students understand what she is talking about. Totally ask questions whenever you don't get something. Homework is fair, the exams were difficult. Grading is fair.
i like it better when i go to her office hours, i understood the material better. she's so fast, sometimes i just can't keep up. but she seems to be happy teaching the course.

Dr. Mishna is very enthused about the material, and it shows. She does tend to go rather quickly, but is available for extra clarification if needed. The material in this class is not easy, and the pace is dictated by the its volume. My guess is most of the complaints come from people who are not used to difficult math classes. Exams were fair.

Very energetic like she took drugs just before class. She talks so fast that it's super hard to keep up. Hands cramp up after every class because of the intensive writing. She gives out bonus points for class participation, but what the hell's the point if she's only going to ask the 1 group that's sitting right in front of her all the time???

Talks way too fast and sometimes what she teaches is completely wrong.  Always gets corrected by the students.  She says the textbook sucks but then her notes are straight out of the book.  Midterms were difficult but the marks were scaled.  Tossed candy into some guy's face.

Must be drinking 4 cups of coffee before each lecture.  I can normally keep up with lecture but this woman is fast!    This also affects her ability to speak, as she randomly spurts different things.  You can't follow by listening to her lecture, you have to be there and attentively watch.  She made the course extremely difficult. Avg midterm: <45%
Enthusiastic, but writes way too fast. The course was hard and she seemed to teach to the top percentile of students.
VERY hard class! she tries her best to teach material but writes too much too fast. it's either write down or understand not both. midterm avg was close to/below 50%.
VERY had class! she tries her best to teach material but writes too much too fast. it's either write down or understand not both. midterm avg was close to/below 50%.
Difficult to keep up with her and copy down her notes at the same time.
No Comments
